1. Paste or enter a Naver Blog post URL
2. Click "Fetch Photos" to extract all images
3. Browse, select and download photos in the gallery

privacy.metaDescription: Privacy policy for Naver Blog Image Downloader — what data we collect, how it is used, and your rights.
privacy.intro: "Naver Blog Image Downloader" is an open-source personal project that operates under a minimum-data-collection principle. No account is required; we do not sell data and do not serve ads. This page explains what we collect, how it is used, and how you can exercise control.

sections.title: Data we collect
body: Naver Blog URLs you submit: sent from the App or Web to our backend service solely to fetch and package images. Submitted URLs are not retained after processing completes.

Anonymous user identifier (Mobile App only): on first launch the App creates an anonymous UID via Firebase Authentication to tie crash reports and operation logs to a device. It contains no email address, phone number, name, or social handle. The session is persisted on-device (iOS Keychain / Android EncryptedSharedPreferences).

Operation logs (Mobile App only): key operations (fetch, download, cache clear, etc.) are written as structured documents to Cloud Firestore at "users/{anonymousUid}/logs/{auto-id}". Fields include operation type, timestamp, related metadata, and device info (OS version, model).
Crash stack traces (Mobile App only): collected automatically by Firebase Crashlytics. Contains stack traces and device info used to fix bugs.

On-device data (not uploaded): locale, theme preference, downloaded-image list, and thumbnail cache are stored on the device only, and can be removed at any time from the settings page via "Clear cache".

sections.title: How we use data
body: To deliver the core feature — fetching, packaging, and downloading Naver Blog images.
To diagnose errors and improve reliability via operation logs.
To locate and fix crashes via crash reports.
We explicitly do not: profile users, serve ads, sell data, or track across apps.

sections.title: Third-party services
body: AWS Lambda + S3 (Amazon Web Services, Inc.): backend packaging pipeline and transient ZIP storage, operated by the project maintainer.
Firebase Authentication (Google LLC): anonymous sign-in; sessions are persisted by the Firebase SDK on device.
Cloud Firestore (Google LLC): stores operation logs keyed by the anonymous UID.
Firebase Crashlytics (Google LLC): collects crash stack traces for debugging.
Naver: the URLs you provide point to Naver Blog; image fetches follow Naver's own cookies and terms of service.

sections.title: Retention and deletion
body: Packaged ZIP files in S3: automatically deleted 1 day after creation via a bucket lifecycle rule on the "jobs/" prefix.
Download links (presigned URLs): valid for 1 hour, expiring automatically thereafter.
Firestore operation logs: not actively purged today; retained per Firebase defaults. To request deletion, contact us via the email below and we will remove all documents under your anonymous UID.
Crashlytics: retained per Firebase's policies.
On-device cache: cleared immediately by the user via the in-app "Clear cache" action.

sections.title: Your rights
body: There is no account system and no "log out" concept. To stop using the product, uninstall the App or stop using the Web.
You can request deletion of all Firestore operation logs tied to your anonymous UID via the contact email below.
You can clear local data at any time from the App settings via "Clear cache".
You can clear this site's Local Storage / Cookies through your browser settings (should any be added in future).

sections.title: Children's privacy
sections.body: This product is not designed for children under 13, and we do not knowingly collect data from them. Age ratings follow the listings on the App Store and Google Play.

sections.title: Policy changes
sections.body: The "Last updated" date on this page is authoritative. Material changes will be announced through GitHub Release Notes and the in-app "What's New" screen.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.body: For questions about this privacy policy, or to request deletion of Firestore operation logs associated with your anonymous UID, please reach out at:
